title: Money stays
subtitle: A two-word affirmation for anchoring the belief that money can remain.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 37 of the MONEY program. For when the assumption underlying every financial decision is that money will eventually leave.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 37 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the default assumption is that any money arriving will be gone by the end of the month.

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 37 of the MONEY program. The two-word version is deliberate: the fear of loss is often a habit so deep it's invisible. A short, clear counter-statement is more useful than a long one.
Money stays. What flows in, stays in. I allow myself to release all fear of sudden loss.
Say it three times, morning or evening. The first two words are the anchor. You can also say just those two words, "money stays", as a reset whenever the fear-of-loss thought shows up during the day.
Adapted from Day 37 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
